lesson: Portfolio Show & Transcript Close — Assemble the Portfolio
essential_question: Gathered together, what does a year of building actually look like?
objective: Gather every build artifact from the year, select the strongest pieces, and record them in the course Portfolio.

content.heading: The transcript's evidence
bullets: A mastery rating is a number; the Portfolio is the thing that was actually made.
Today's job: find everything — every build, every photo, every write-up from this year.
Tomorrow you'll present it. First, you have to find it all.

content.heading: Search the whole year
content.note: Start with the four capstones — Week 12 (Unit 01), Week 22 (Unit 02), Week 35 (Unit 03), and Week 39 (Year-End Capstone Build) — then scan every weekly S2 · Build log in The Salas Compass.

content.preview: Session 2: showcase — present your year's builds.

success_criteria.text: I can locate and list the build artifacts from across the year
I can select and describe my strongest builds, including all four capstones
I can record my best build in the course Portfolio

compass.note: Curriculum/Geometry/The Salas Compass/2026-W40 Portfolio Show & Transcript Close.md

questions.prompt: List every build artifact you can find from this year — a photo, an STL file, a CAD screenshot, a PDF write-up. Start with the four capstones (Weeks 12, 22, 35, and 39), then scan your weekly S2 · Build logs in The Salas Compass.
Pick your single best build from the year. Name it and say in one sentence why it best shows what you can do.
For each of the four capstones — Week 12, 22, 35, and 39 — write one sentence describing what you built and what math it proved.
Open [[Records/Portfolio]] and add a row for your best build with its artifact and a short note. Confirm you did it.
Exit: what's missing from your portfolio that you wish you'd photographed or saved along the way?
